dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, dispatch for breathing problems and vomiting at 5125 Armistead Road.
Hi, I'm Dr. Milestone with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I've been throwing up all day and my stomach hurts.
Are you having trouble breathing as well, or is the chest pain worse than the stomach pain?
It's not really my chest-mostly my belly. It feels 10 out of 10 right now.
Can you open your eyes and tell me your name, please?
Janice. Janice Quinn.
Good, Ms. Quinn. You know who you are. Can you tell me where we are?
At my house. 5125 Armistead Road.
Squeeze my fingers tightly for me now.

There we go! 12. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: signs and symptoms; EMT: When did you first start vomiting? 13. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: signs and symptoms; Patient: It started last night while I was asleep.

dialogue.micro_intent: events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What were you doing or eating the night before you started vomiting? 15. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: events leading up to the event; Patient: I had spaghetti and red wine with a friend. He said I maybe drank too much, but I thought it was fine. 16. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: events leading up to the event; EMT: Any nausea, dizziness, or fever with that vomiting?

dialogue.micro_intent: events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: No-just the throwing up and pain in the belly. 18. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: allergies; EMT: Any medication or food allergies?

I'm allergic to penicillin. 20. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: medications; EMT: What medications are you currently taking? 21. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: medications; Patient: Seroquel and Klonopin.
Do you have any existing medical conditions?
I have schizoaffective disorder.
Any food or drink since the vomiting started?
No. I haven't been able to keep anything down.
What's the worst your abdominal pain has felt today on a 0 to 10 scale?
Airway is patent and clear.
Regular breathing, no signs of distress.
Pulse is strong and regular.
Skin appears warm and pink.
Heart rate is 107 and it's regular.
Respirations are 18 per minute.
Blood pressure is 154 over 77.
Blood sugar is 133 at this time.
Oxygen saturation is 97% on room air.
Placed 4-lead monitoring now for continuous tracking.
Alert and oriented to self, place, and time.
Pupils are equal and reactive to light.
Abdomen is soft and nontender upon visual inspection.
Performing 12-lead ECG for rhythm assessment now.
ECG shows sinus tachycardia and moderate left axis deviation, plus occasional premature complexes.
Now, blood pressure is 171 over 105.
Heart rate is now 103 with good rhythm.
Breathing is still regular at 19 per minute.
Oxygen saturation remains at 100%.
Pulse is at 101 and rhythm is still regular.
Oxygen saturation is now 98%. Respirations have dropped to 16 per minute.
Blood pressure is now 136 over 92.
No noticeable EKG rhythm changes so far.
Is the pain staying at 10, or has it improved at all?
Not much-still 10 out of 10, but just one vomiting episode since you arrived.
Any new sensations or movement discomfort elsewhere?
No. It's still just the stomach pain.
Given the findings, we are following the Medical-Abdominal Pain protocol.
You look okay for travel. We're going to [CITY].
We're now helping you up to the stair chair.
Positioning you in semi-Fowler's for comfort and to monitor posture during transport.
All straps are fastened. Patient is secured and ready for transport.
Vitals and heart monitor are stable in transit conditions.
All right. We have a clear way to the stretcher.
Transferred to the stretcher without incident. Secured fully there.
On the semi-Fowler's with support-should help with comfort and breathing.
Pulsation is 101 and consistent. All good signs for travel.
Vitals are stable and normal for the patient in current condition.
No signs of increasing discomfort or dizziness while in transit.
I'm doing just fine and holding it in.
You are A&Ox4 to person, place, time, and purpose.
ABCs are self-maintained by the patient at this time.
Respirations are now 16 per minute.
Still reading 0 due to cold blood sample. Might recheck if needed.
Just double-checking-how's the pain now? Any change?
No-just the same, but nobody else is touching me.
Using the mattress supports and Fowlers' to ease discomfort as much as possible.
All equipment ready. Safe to transport now.
No patient distress-monitors are steady. Ready to arrive.
Blood pressure remains unchanged at 136 over 92 and heart rate 101.
Arrived at [COMPANY_NAME]. Waiting on receiving.
She has a 12-lead ECG done-sinus tach with moderate left axis deviation and nonspecific T wave abnormality.
She vomited once during EMS contact-no signs of trauma or AMS.
GCS 15 with full orientation and no LOC reported. ABCs were self-kept throughout.
Past medical history of schizoaffective disorder-no allergies but penicillin allergy confirmed.
Blood glucose at 0 due to cold finger, but no IV started-will recheck per orders.
Looks stable, able to travel, and holding in. No need for more interventions now.
All clear for final handoff. Safe transition.
Handoff complete. All equipment accounted for at site.
